items.title: Virtual environments and dependencies: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Python: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Activating a virtual environment changes Python globally and guarantees every editor, task, and subprocess uses it.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. A virtual environment is selected by its interpreter path; activation is a shell convenience, so verify the executable used by every tool.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. A virtual environment is selected by its interpreter path; activation is a shell convenience, so verify the executable used by every tool.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.
